title: Mock-workers schema
description: Look up the live mock-worker configuration JSON Schema—definitions, fields, constraints, defaults, enums, and examples—on a stable deep-linkable docs route.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference shows the live mock-worker configuration JSON Schema from the you-agent-factory API package. Use it to look up definitions, fields, constraints, defaults, enums, and examples for mock-worker dispatch configuration. Field and definition descriptions come from the package schema itself.

schemaLookup.title: Schema Lookup
schemaLookup.body: Browse the complete mock-workers schema below. Filter by definition or field name, follow $ref links to related definitions, and copy deep links to any definition or field anchor on this page.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start from the root mock-worker configuration object, then open nested definitions when a field points at another schema. Use the filter to find a field or definition by name. Copy a deep link when you need to share a stable pointer into this page. Contract text on fields and definitions stays in the package schema language even when the page chrome is localized.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page covers the mock-worker configuration package schema only. It is not the Factory schema, the You-config schema, or authored Worker documentation such as /docs/workers/mock. Use this lookup for the JSON Schema contract; use Worker pages for how mock workers behave in the factory. If the schema cannot be loaded, the lookup surface shows an explicit unavailable state instead of an empty page.
